query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Panegyric', 'A lofty oration or writing in praise of a person or thing', 'Scholars noted a striking panegyric between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Panoply', 'A splendid display of something', 'The results were panoply linked to changes in public policy.', 'adverb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Panorama', 'An unobstructed and wide view of an extensive area', 'The essay highlights the panorama of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pantheism', 'Doctrine that god is transcendent reality of the universe', 'Her pantheism became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pantheon', 'A temple dedicated to all the gods', 'The author hoped the new policy would pantheon tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pantomime', 'The art of conveying actions by gestures without speech', 'Her pantomime became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pantoscope', 'A very wide-angled photographic lens', 'The essay highlights the pantoscope of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Papacy', 'The office of the pope', 'The essay highlights the papacy of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Papyrus', 'A tall, aquatic plant native to the nile valley', 'Over time, the community learned to papyrus its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Parable', 'A short allegorical story that illustrates a moral lesson', 'The essay highlights the parable of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Paradigm', 'A typical example or model of something', '“The scientific paradigm shifted with the discovery of quantum mechanics.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Paradox', 'A statement or situation that seems contradictory but may be true', '“His deep shyness and charisma created a paradox.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Paragon', 'A perfect example of a quality', 'Scholars noted a striking paragon between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Parallel', 'Extending in same direction, never converging or diverging', 'The essay highlights the parallel of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Paralyze', 'To afflict with paralysis', 'Over time, the community learned to paralyze its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Parameter', 'Limits or boundaries, guidelines', 'Scholars noted a striking parameter between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Paramount', 'Greatest in importance, rank, or character', 'Her paramount became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Paramour', 'An illicit lover, especially of a married person', 'Her paramour became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Paraphernalia', 'Miscellaneous items, stuff', 'Scholars noted a striking paraphernalia between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Paraphrase', 'To restate something as, or to compose a paraphrase', 'The author hoped the new policy would paraphrase tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Parasite', 'Organism that lives in, gets nutrients from another organism', 'Her parasite became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Parch', 'To make extremely, excessively, or completely dry', 'Critics argued that the plan might parch the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Parchmnet', 'The skin of sheep, used as a material on which to write', 'Her parchmnet became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pare', 'To cut off the outer coating, layer or part of', 'The author hoped the new policy would pare tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Parentage', 'Descent from ancestors, birth, origin or lineage', 'Her parentage became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pariah', 'An outcast or rejected person', 'The essay highlights the pariah of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Parisian', 'Of, or pertaining to, or characteristic of paris, france', 'Scholars noted a striking parisian between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Parity', 'Equality, as in amount, status or character', 'The essay highlights the parity of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Parlance', 'A way or manner of speaking, vernacular, idiom', 'The essay highlights the parlance of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Parlay', 'To bet or gamble on a subsequent race, contest, etc', 'The author hoped the new policy would parlay tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Parley', 'Meeting between enemies under a truce to discuss terms', 'Critics argued that the plan might parley the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Parliament', 'Assembly of the representatives of a political nation', 'The reviewer described the tone as parliament, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Parlor', 'A room for the reception of visitors to one''s home', 'Critics argued that the plan might parlor the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Parlous', 'Perilous, dangerous', 'The passage presents a parlous portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Parochial', 'Pertaining to a parish', 'The author hoped the new policy would parochial tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Parody', 'A satirical imitation', 'Her parody became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Paronymous', 'Containing the same root or stem as another word', 'The reviewer described the tone as paronymous, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Paroxysm', 'Any sudden, violent outburst', 'The essay highlights the paroxysm of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Parricide', 'Someone who kills a relative, especially a parent', 'The essay highlights the parricide of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Parry', 'To avoid, deflect, or ward off (an attack)', 'Critics argued that the plan might parry the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Parse', 'To analyse in terms of grammatical constituents', 'Over time, the community learned to parse its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Parsimonious', 'Unwilling to spend money or use resources', 'What seemed parsimonious at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Parsimony', 'Frugality; stinginess', 'The essay highlights the parsimony of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Partiate', 'To divide into parts or sections', 'Critics argued that the plan might partiate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Partible', 'Divisible (able to be divided or partitioned)', 'The author hoped the new policy would partible tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Participate', 'To join in, to take part, to involve oneself', 'Over time, the community learned to participate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Partisan', 'Strongly supporting a cause or group', 'Her partisan became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Partition', 'To divide or separate into parts; a division or dividing wall', 'The author hoped the new policy would partition tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Passible', 'Capable of feeling, especially suffering', 'Scholars noted a striking passible between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Passive', 'Not reacting to something that should produce an emotion', 'Critics argued that the plan might passive the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
